basic_01_inventory_cash.reason: Dr Inventory $100 / Cr Cash $100. Exact match with expected entry.

basic_02_inventory_on_account.reason: Expected Dr Inventories $300 / Cr Trade payables $300. Actual adds HST Receivable $39 debit and inflates AP to $339. Core accounts and amounts correct; differences are solely tax-related (added HST receivable and tax-adjusted AP).

basic_03_issue_stock_with_apic.reason: Dr Cash $180 / Cr Common Stock $100 / Cr Additional Paid-In Capital $80. Account names semantically equivalent to expected (Share capital-Common = Common Stock, APIC = Additional Paid-In Capital). Amounts exact.

basic_04_sell_inventory.reason: Expected Dr Cash $500 / Dr COGS $300 / Cr Revenue $500 / Cr Inventories $300. Actual adds HST Payable $65 credit and inflates Cash to $565. Core non-tax lines match; differences are solely HST-related (added tax collection on sale).

basic_05_pay_accounts_payable.reason: Dr Accounts Payable $50 / Cr Cash $50. Accounts Payable = Trade payables. Amounts exact.

basic_06_refinance_loan.reason: Dr Bank Loan Payable (Old) $100 / Cr Bank Loan Payable (New) $100. Both are Notes payable equivalents. Amounts exact.

basic_07_loan_to_equity.reason: Dr Bank Loan Payable $200 / Cr Common Shares $200. Bank Loan Payable = Notes payable, Common Shares = Share capital-Common. Amounts exact.

basic_08_deliver_service_against_deposit.reason: Dr Customer Deposits $100 / Cr Service Revenue $100. Customer Deposits = Contract liabilities, Service Revenue = Revenue. Amounts exact.

basic_09_repurchase_stock.reason: Dr Treasury Stock $200 / Cr Cash $200. Treasury Stock = Treasury shares. Amounts exact.

basic_10_declare_dividend.reason: Dr Dividends Declared $500,000 / Cr Dividends Payable $500,000. Dividends Declared is a contra-equity account that reduces Retained earnings; semantically equivalent. Amounts exact.

basic_11_convert_preferred_to_common.reason: Dr Preferred Shares $100 / Cr Common Shares $100. Preferred Shares = Share capital-Preferred, Common Shares = Share capital-Common. Amounts exact.

basic_12_pay_salaries.reason: Dr Salaries Expense $100 / Cr Cash $100. Exact semantic match. Amounts exact.

basic_13_accrue_utility.reason: Dr Utilities Expense $50 / Cr Accrued Liabilities $50. Exact semantic match with expected. Amounts exact.

basic_14_casualty_loss.reason: Dr Loss from Casualty $2,000 / Cr Inventory $2,000. Loss from Casualty = Casualty loss. Amounts exact.

basic_15_no_entry.reason: Expected null entry. Actual journal_entry is null. Correct no-entry decision.

int_03_machinery_purchase.reason: Expected Dr PP&E Machinery $800,000 / Cr Trade payables $700,000 / Cr Cash $100,000. Actual has correct PP&E $800,000 but adds HST Receivable $104,000 and inflates AP to $791,000 and Cash to $113,000. Core non-tax lines match; differences are solely HST-related.

int_04_major_overhaul.reason: Dr PP&E Machinery $200,000 / Cr Cash $200,000. Exact match. Amounts exact.

int_05_bond_issuance_discount.reason: Expected Dr Cash $2,657,510 / Dr Discount on bonds payable $342,490 / Cr Bonds payable $3,000,000. Actual has Cash $2,566,305 / Discount $433,695. The PV calculation differs significantly ($91K gap) — actual used 3-period annual discounting while expected likely used semi-annual periods. This is a computational error, not a rounding difference.

int_06_warranty_provision.reason: Dr Warranty Expense $900,000 / Cr Warranty Provision $900,000. Warranty Provision = Provision for warranties. Amounts exact.

int_07_decommissioning_provision.reason: Dr PP&E Marine Structure $4,000,000 + Dr PP&E Asset Retirement Cost $1,950,000 = $5,950,000 total PP&E debit (matching expected $5,950,000). Splitting the ARO capitalized component into a separate PP&E sub-account is an accepted practice under IAS 16/IFRIC 1. Credits: AP $4,000,000 = Trade payables, Provision for ARO $1,950,000 = Decommissioning provision. All amounts exact.

int_08_share_repurchase_cancel.reason: Dr Common Shares $50,000 / Dr Retained Earnings $10,000 / Cr Cash $60,000. Common Shares = Share capital. Amounts exact.

int_09_merchandise_with_costs.reason: Expected Dr Inventories $1,150,000 / Dr Warehousing expense $20,000 / Dr Distribution costs $50,000 / Cr Trade payables $1,000,000 / Cr Cash $220,000. Actual has correct inventory, expense accounts (Warehouse Storage = Warehousing, Delivery = Distribution), and amounts, but adds HST Receivable lines ($149,500 + $2,600 + $6,500) and adjusts AP to $1,149,500 and Cash to $228,600. Core non-tax lines match; differences solely tax-related.

int_10_prepayment.reason: Dr Advances to Suppliers $1,000,000 / Cr Cash $1,000,000. Advances to Suppliers = Prepayments (both are current asset accounts for advance payments). Amounts exact.

int_11_land_instalment_discount.reason: Expected Dr Land $49,173,000 / Dr Discount on long-term payables $10,827,000 / Cr Long-term payables $60,000,000. Actual records Dr Land $49,173,000 / Cr Notes Payable $49,173,000 — omits the Discount on long-term payables and records the liability at PV instead of face value. The net liability is the same but presentation differs: expected uses gross method (face value liability + contra discount), actual uses net method. Land amount matches.

int_12_site_improvements.reason: Expected Dr PP&E Site improvements $1,750,000 / Dr Land $2,360,000 / Cr Cash $1,750,000 / Cr Cash-chequing $2,360,000. Actual uses 'PP&E Land Improvements (Depreciable)' for $1,750,000 (acceptable for site improvements) and 'PP&E Land Improvements (Non-Depreciable)' for $2,360,000 (should be Land, not land improvements). The second debit account misclassifies: expected is Land, actual is a PP&E land improvement sub-account. Credit side: Cash $1,750,000 matches, Bank $2,360,000 is semantically equivalent to Cash-chequing.

int_13_donation_note.reason: Dr Charitable Donations Expense $1,000,000 / Cr Notes Payable $1,000,000. Charitable Donations Expense = Donations expense. Amounts exact.

int_14_vehicle_mixed_payment.reason: Dr PP&E Vehicles $40,000 / Cr Cash $20,000 / Cr Notes Payable $20,000. Exact match. Amounts exact.

int_15_bank_loan.reason: Dr Cash $200,000 / Cr Bank Loan Payable $200,000. Bank Loan Payable = Long-term borrowings. Amounts exact.

int_17_customer_deposit.reason: Dr Cash $5,000 / Cr Deferred Revenue-Customer Deposits $5,000. Cash = Cash-chequing (semantically equivalent). Deferred Revenue-Customer Deposits = Contract liabilities. Amounts exact.

int_18_multiple_assets.reason: Expected Dr PP&E Office equipment $5,500 / Dr PP&E Vehicles $250,000 / Cr Cash $5,500 / Cr Trade payables $250,000. Actual splits office equipment into Furniture $2,000 and Computer Equipment $3,500 (totaling $5,500) and adds HST Receivable $33,215, inflating Cash to $6,215 and AP to $282,500. The office equipment split is more granular but total matches. Tax-relaxed: removing HST lines and adjusting amounts gives core match.

int_19_service_revenue_cash.reason: Expected Dr Cash $25,500 / Cr Revenue-Service revenue $25,500. Actual adds HST Payable $3,315 and inflates Cash to $28,815. Core revenue amount $25,500 matches; differences solely tax-related (HST collected on services).

int_20_service_revenue_credit.reason: Expected Dr Trade receivables $31,500 / Cr Revenue-Service revenue $31,500. Actual adds HST Payable $4,095 and inflates AR to $35,595. Core revenue $31,500 matches; differences solely tax-related.

int_21_rd_expense.reason: Expected Dr R&D expense $50,000 / Cr Cash-chequing $50,000. Actual adds HST Receivable $6,500 and inflates Cash to $56,500. R&D expense $50,000 matches. Cash vs Cash-chequing acceptable. Differences solely tax-related.

int_22_compound_sale_with_tax.reason: Actual: Dr Cash $45,900 / Dr AR $30,000 / Dr COGS $55,500 / Cr HST Payable $6,900 / Cr Sales Revenue $69,000 / Cr Inventory $55,500. Expected uses Cash-chequing and Sales tax payable. HST Payable = Sales tax payable, Cash = Cash-chequing. All amounts exact. The expected entry explicitly includes the tax line.

int_23_split_electricity.reason: Expected Dr WIP Manufacturing overhead $15,000 / Dr Utilities expense $5,500 / Cr Credit card payable $20,500. Actual adds HST Receivable $2,665 and inflates Credit Card Payable to $23,165. Manufacturing Overhead = WIP Manufacturing overhead. Core non-tax amounts match; differences solely tax-related.

int_24_advertising.reason: Expected Dr Advertising expense $22,000 / Cr Cash $22,000. Actual splits into Marketing Expense $19,469.03 + HST Receivable $2,530.97 / Cr Cash $22,000. The actual treats the $22,000 as tax-inclusive and backs out HST. Expected treats it as pre-tax. Cash total matches. Tax-relaxed: the difference is entirely tax treatment.

int_25_building_purchase_allocation.reason: Dr Land $6,000,000 / Dr Buildings $3,000,000 / Dr HST Receivable $300,000 / Cr AP $4,500,000 / Cr Cash $4,800,000. HST Receivable = VAT receivable, Buildings = Building, Cash = Cash-chequing, Accounts Payable = Other payables. All amounts exact match with expected.

int_26a_payroll_recognition.reason: Dr Manufacturing Wages $25,000 (=WIP Direct labour) / Dr Administrative Salaries $20,000 (=Salaries expense) / Cr withholdings $7,750 total (Pension $2,000 + Health $3,250 + EI $1,050 + Income Tax $1,450 = $7,750 = Statutory withholdings payable) / Cr Cash $37,250 (=Cash-chequing). All amounts match. The 4 disaggregated withholding accounts total to the expected $7,750 aggregate.

int_26b_payroll_remittance.reason: Expected Dr Statutory withholdings payable $7,750 / Dr Employee benefits expense $6,300 / Cr Cash $14,050. Actual debits 4 separate payable accounts (Pension $4,000, Health $6,500, EI $2,100, Income Tax $1,450 = $14,050) / Cr Cash $14,050. Actual total debit is $14,050 but does not include any Employee benefits expense line (the employer-matching portion). The actual entry only clears liabilities without recognizing the employer's additional benefit cost of $6,300. This is a substantive error — missing expense recognition.

int_28_promotional_literature.reason: Expected Dr Advertising expense $7,000 / Cr Cash $7,000. Actual records Dr Promotional Materials (asset) $7,000 / Cr Cash $7,000. The actual capitalizes the cost as an asset instead of expensing it as advertising. This is a classification difference — expense vs asset.

int_29_security_deposit_received.reason: Dr Cash $25,000 / Cr Security Deposits Payable $25,000. Security Deposits Payable = Rental deposits received. Amounts exact.

int_30_short_term_loan_advance.reason: Dr Loans Receivable-Short-term $2,000,000 / Cr Cash-Chequing $2,000,000. Short-term loans receivable equivalent. Cash-Chequing matches expected. Amounts exact.

int_31_vehicle_nonrecoverable_tax.reason: Dr PP&E Vehicles $52,800 / Cr Notes Payable-Ford Dealer $52,800. Notes Payable = Other payables (both are liability accounts for the dealer financing). Amounts exact. Non-recoverable tax correctly capitalized into asset cost.

int_hard_01a_note_derecognition.reason: Dr Cash $98,356.16 / Dr Loss on Discounting $1,643.84 / Cr Notes Receivable $100,000. Expected Cash $98,356 / Loss $1,644 / Cr Trade receivables $100,000. Amounts within minor rounding ($0.16). Loss on Discounting = Loss on derecognition. Notes Receivable = Trade receivables (the underlying instrument). Match within rounding tolerance.

int_hard_01b_note_collateralized.reason: Dr Cash $98,356.16 / Dr Interest Expense $1,643.84 / Cr Loan Payable-Bank $100,000. Expected Cash $98,356 / Interest expense $1,644 / Cr Short-term borrowings $100,000. Amounts within rounding. Loan Payable = Short-term borrowings.

int_hard_02a_investment_fvtpl.reason: Dr Financial Assets at FVTPL $3,000,000 / Dr Transaction Fee Expense $100,000 / Cr Cash $3,100,000. Transaction Fee Expense = Investment transaction costs. Amounts exact.

int_hard_02b_investment_fvoci.reason: Dr Investment in Equity Securities-FVOCI $3,100,000 / Cr Cash $3,100,000. Financial assets at FVOCI = Investment in Equity Securities-FVOCI. Amounts exact.

int_hard_02c_investment_equity_method.reason: Dr Investment in Associate-Ford $3,100,000 / Cr Cash $3,100,000. Investment in associate matches expected. Amounts exact.

int_hard_27a_meal_overtime.reason: Expected Dr Employee benefits expense $125 / Cr Credit card payable $125. Actual adds HST Receivable $16.25 and inflates Credit Card Payable to $141.25. Core expense account and amount ($125) match. Differences solely tax-related.

int_hard_27b_meal_meeting.reason: Expected Dr Meeting expense $125 / Cr Credit card payable $125. Actual uses Meals and Entertainment Expense $125 (different account name) + HST Receivable $16.25 / Cr Credit Card Payable $141.25. Account name differs (Meals & Entertainment vs Meeting expense), but the core amount matches. Tax-relaxed: the tax lines explain the credit card amount difference, and Meals & Entertainment is a realistic alternative classification for a meeting meal.

int_hard_27c_meal_entertainment.reason: Expected Dr Entertainment expense $125 / Cr Credit card payable $125. Actual uses Meals and Entertainment Expense $125 + HST Receivable $16.25 / Cr Credit Card Payable $141.25. Meals and Entertainment Expense is semantically equivalent to Entertainment expense for a meal. Tax-relaxed: differences solely tax-related.

int_hard_27d_meal_factory.reason: Dr Manufacturing Overhead $125 / Cr Credit Card Payable $125. Manufacturing Overhead = WIP Manufacturing overhead. Amounts exact.

int_hard_32a_grocery_entertainment.reason: Expected Dr Entertainment expense $1,320 / Cr Credit card payable $1,320. Actual has Dr Meals and Entertainment Expense $1,200 + HST Receivable $120 / Cr Credit Card Payable $1,320. The expected $1,320 includes tax in the expense. Actual separates out tax ($120) reducing expense to $1,200. Credit card total matches at $1,320. Meals and Entertainment = Entertainment expense. Tax-relaxed: difference is solely tax treatment (HST separated vs embedded in expense).

int_hard_32b_grocery_breakroom.reason: Expected Dr Employee benefits expense $1,320 / Cr Credit card payable $1,320. Actual has Dr Employee Welfare Expense $1,200 + HST Receivable $120 / Cr Credit Card Payable $1,320. Employee Welfare Expense = Employee benefits expense. Expected includes tax in expense ($1,320), actual separates HST ($120). Credit card total $1,320 matches. Tax-relaxed: difference is solely tax treatment.

int_hard_16a_rent_prepaid.reason: Dr Prepaid Rent $24,000 / Cr Cash $24,000. Expected Cr Cash-chequing; Cash = Cash-chequing. Amounts exact.

int_hard_16b_rent_expense.reason: Expected Dr Rent expense $24,000 / Cr Cash-chequing $24,000. Actual records Dr Prepaid Rent $24,000 + Dr Rent Expense $2,000 + Dr HST Receivable $3,380 / Cr Cash $29,380. The actual incorrectly splits between prepaid and expense (only $2K expensed vs full $24K expected), adds HST, and inflates the cash payment. The expected entry expenses the full amount immediately. This is a substantive classification and amount error beyond just tax differences.
